children.heading: Deduction allowed
children.label: a Deduction allowed
children.text: In the case of an individual who is an eligible individual for any month during the taxable year, there shall be allowed as a deduction for the taxable year an amount equal to the aggregate amount paid in cash during such taxable year by or on behalf of such individual to a health savings account of such individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount allowable as a deduction under subsection (a) to an individual for the taxable year shall not exceed the sum of the monthly limitations for months during such taxable year that the individual is an eligible individual.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of an eligible individual who has self-only coverage under a high deductible health plan as of the first day of such month, $2,250.

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an eligible individual who has family coverage under a high deductible health plan as of the first day of such month, $4,500.

children.heading: Additional contributions for individuals 55 or older
children.label: 3 Additional contributions for individuals 55 or older

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of an individual who has attained age 55 before the close of the taxable year, the applicable limitation under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (2) shall be increased by the additional contribution amount.

children.heading: Additional contribution amount
children.label: B Additional contribution amount
children.text: For purposes of this section, the additional contribution amount is the amount determined in accordance with the following table:For taxable years beginning in:The additional contribution amount is:2004$5002005$6002006$7002007$8002008$9002009 and thereafter$1,000.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate amount paid for such taxable year to Archer MSAs of such individual,

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount contributed to health savings accounts of such individual which is excludable from the taxpayer’s gross income for such taxable year under section 106(d) (and such amount shall not be allowed as a deduction under subsection (a)), and

children.label: C
children.text: the aggregate amount contributed to health savings accounts of such individual for such taxable year under section 408(d)(9) (and such amount shall not be allowed as a deduction under subsection (a)).

children.label: A
children.text: both spouses shall be treated as having only such family coverage (and if such spouses each have family coverage under different plans, as having the family coverage with the lowest annual deductible), and

children.label: i
children.text: shall be reduced by the aggregate amount paid to Archer MSAs of such spouses for the taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: after such reduction, shall be divided equally between them unless they agree on a different division.

children.heading: Denial of deduction to dependents
children.label: 6 Denial of deduction to dependents
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this section to any individual with respect to whom a deduction under section 151 is allowable to another taxpayer for a taxable year beginning in the calendar year in which such individual’s taxable year begins.

children.heading: Medicare eligible individuals
children.label: 7 Medicare eligible individuals
children.text: The limitation under this subsection for any month with respect to an individual shall be zero for the first month such individual is entitled to benefits under title XVIII of the Social Security Act and for each month thereafter.

children.heading: Increase in limit for individuals becoming eligible individuals after the beginning of the year
children.label: 8 Increase in limit for individuals becoming eligible individuals after the beginning of the year
i
children.text: as having been an eligible individual during each of the months in such taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as having been enrolled, during each of the months such individual is treated as an eligible individual solely by reason of clause (i), in the same high deductible health plan in which the individual was enrolled for the last month of such taxable year.

children.heading: Failure to maintain high deductible health plan coverage
children.label: B Failure to maintain high deductible health plan coverage
I
children.text: gross income of the individual for the taxable year in which occurs the first month in the testing period for which such individual is not an eligible individual is increased by the aggregate amount of all contributions to the health savings account of the individual which could not have been made but for subparagraph (A), and

children.label: II
children.text: the tax imposed by this chapter for any taxable year on the individual shall be increased by 10 percent of the amount of such increase.

children.heading: Exception for disability or death
children.label: ii Exception for disability or death
children.text: Subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i) shall not apply if the individual ceased to be an eligible individual by reason of the death of the individual or the individual becoming disabled (within the meaning of section 72(m)(7)).

children.heading: Testing period
children.label: iii Testing period
children.text: The term “testing period” means the period beginning with the last month of the taxable year referred to in subparagraph (A) and ending on the last day of the 12th month following such month.

children.label: i
children.text: such individual is covered under a high deductible health plan as of the 1st day of such month, and

children.label: I
children.text: which is not a high deductible health plan, and

children.label: II
children.text: which provides coverage for any benefit which is covered under the high deductible health plan.

children.label: i
children.text: coverage for any benefit provided by permitted insurance,

children.label: ii
children.text: coverage (whether through insurance or otherwise) for accidents, disability, dental care, vision care, long-term care, or telehealth and other remote care, and

children.label: I
children.text: the balance in such arrangement at the end of such plan year is zero, or

children.label: II
children.text: the individual is making a qualified HSA distribution (as defined in section 106(e)) in an amount equal to the remaining balance in such arrangement as of the end of such plan year, in accordance with rules prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Special rule for individuals eligible for certain veterans benefits
children.label: C Special rule for individuals eligible for certain veterans benefits
children.text: An individual shall not fail to be treated as an eligible individual for any period merely because the individual receives hospital care or medical services under any law administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for a service-connected disability (within the meaning of , United States Code).section 101(16) of title 38

children.heading: Special rule for individuals receiving benefits subject to surprise billing statutes
children.label: D Special rule for individuals receiving benefits subject to surprise billing statutes
children.text: An individual shall not fail to be treated as an eligible individual for any period merely because the individual receives benefits for medical care subject to and in accordance with section 9816 or 9817, section 2799A–1 or 2799A–2 of the Public Health Service Act, or section 716 or 717 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, or any State law providing similar protections to such individual.

children.heading: Treatment of direct primary care service arrangements
children.label: E Treatment of direct primary care service arrangements

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A direct primary care service arrangement shall not be treated as a health plan for purposes of subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Direct primary care service arrangement
children.label: ii Direct primary care service arrangement

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “direct primary care service arrangement” means, with respect to any individual, an arrangement under which such individual is provided medical care (as defined in section 213(d)) consisting solely of primary care services provided by primary care practitioners (as defined in section 1833(x)(2)(A) of the Social Security Act, determined without regard to clause (ii) thereof), if the sole compensation for such care is a fixed periodic fee.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: II Limitation
children.text: With respect to any individual for any month, such term shall not include any arrangement if the aggregate fees for all direct primary care service arrangements (determined without regard to this subclause) with respect to such individual for such month exceed $150 (twice such dollar amount in the case of an individual with any direct primary care service arrangement (as so determined) that covers more than one individual).

children.heading: Certain services specifically excluded from treatment as primary care services
children.label: iii Certain services specifically excluded from treatment as primary care services
I
children.text: procedures that require the use of general anesthesia,

children.label: II
children.text: prescription drugs (other than vaccines), and

children.label: III
children.text: laboratory services not typically administered in an ambulatory primary care setting.

children.label: I
children.text: $1,000 for self-only coverage, and

children.label: II
children.text: twice the dollar amount in subclause (I) for family coverage, and

children.label: I
children.text: $5,000 for self-only coverage, and

children.label: II
children.text: twice the dollar amount in subclause (I) for family coverage.

children.heading: Exclusion of certain plans
children.label: B Exclusion of certain plans
children.text: Such term does not include a health plan if substantially all of its coverage is coverage described in paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Safe harbor for absence of preventive care deductible
children.label: C Safe harbor for absence of preventive care deductible
children.text: A plan shall not fail to be treated as a high deductible health plan by reason of failing to have a deductible for preventive care (within the meaning of section 1861 of the Social Security Act, except as otherwise provided by the Secretary).

children.heading: Special rules for network plans
children.label: D Special rules for network plans

children.heading: Annual out-of-pocket limitation
children.label: i Annual out-of-pocket limitation
children.text: Such plan shall not fail to be treated as a high deductible health plan by reason of having an out-of-pocket limitation for services provided outside of such network which exceeds the applicable limitation under subparagraph (A)(ii).

children.heading: Annual deductible
children.label: ii Annual deductible
children.text: Such plan’s annual deductible for services provided outside of such network shall not be taken into account for purposes of subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Safe harbor for absence of deductible for telehealth
children.label: E Safe harbor for absence of deductible for telehealth
children.text: A plan shall not fail to be treated as a high deductible health plan by reason of failing to have a deductible for telehealth and other remote care services.

children.heading: Special rule for surprise billing
children.label: F Special rule for surprise billing
children.text: A plan shall not fail to be treated as a high deductible health plan by reason of providing benefits for medical care in accordance with section 9816 or 9817, section 2799A–1 or 2799A–2 of the Public Health Service Act, or section 716 or 717 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, or any State law providing similar protections to individuals, prior to the satisfaction of the deductible under paragraph (2)(A)(i).

children.heading: Safe harbor for absence of deductible for certain insulin products
children.label: G Safe harbor for absence of deductible for certain insulin products

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A plan shall not fail to be treated as a high deductible health plan by reason of failing to have a deductible for selected insulin products.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “selected insulin products” means any dosage form (such as vial, pump, or inhaler dosage forms) of any different type (such as rapid-acting, short-acting, intermediate-acting, long-acting, ultra long-acting, and premixed) of insulin.

children.heading: Insulin
children.label: II Insulin
children.text: The term “insulin” means insulin that is licensed under subsection (a) or (k) of section 351 of the Public Health Service Act () and continues to be marketed under such section, including any insulin product that has been deemed to be licensed under section 351(a) of such Act pursuant to section 7002(e)(4) of the Biologics Price Competition and Innovation Act of 2009 () and continues to be marketed pursuant to such licensure.42 U.S.C. 262Public Law 111–148

children.heading: Bronze and catastrophic plans treated as high deductible health plans
children.label: H Bronze and catastrophic plans treated as high deductible health plans
i
children.text: available as individual coverage through an Exchange established under section 1311 or 1321 of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act, and

children.label: ii
children.text: described in subsection (d)(1)(A) or (e) of section 1302 of such Act.

children.label: i
children.text: liabilities incurred under workers’ compensation laws,

children.label: ii
children.text: tort liabilities,

children.label: iii
children.text: liabilities relating to ownership or use of property, or

children.label: iv
children.text: such other similar liabilities as the Secretary may specify by regulations,

children.label: B
children.text: insurance for a specified disease or illness, and

children.label: C
children.text: insurance paying a fixed amount per day (or other period) of hospitalization.

children.heading: Family coverage
children.label: 4 Family coverage
children.text: The term “family coverage” means any coverage other than self-only coverage.

children.heading: Archer MSA
children.label: 5 Archer MSA
children.text: The term “Archer MSA” has the meaning given such term in section 220(d).

children.label: i
children.text: unless it is in cash, or

children.label: I
children.text: the dollar amount in effect under subsection (b)(2)(B), and

children.label: II
children.text: the dollar amount in effect under subsection (b)(3)(B).

children.label: B
children.text: The trustee is a bank (as defined in section 408(n)), an insurance company (as defined in section 816), or another person who demonstrates to the satisfaction of the Secretary that the manner in which such person will administer the trust will be consistent with the requirements of this section.

children.label: C
children.text: No part of the trust assets will be invested in life insurance contracts.

children.label: D
children.text: The assets of the trust will not be commingled with other property except in a common trust fund or common investment fund.

children.label: E
children.text: The interest of an individual in the balance in his account is nonforfeitable.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified medical expenses” means, with respect to an account beneficiary, amounts paid by such beneficiary for medical care (as defined in section 213(d)) for such individual, the spouse of such individual, and any dependent (as defined in section 152, determined without regard to subsections (b)(1), (b)(2), and (d)(1)(B) thereof) of such individual, but only to the extent such amounts are not compensated for by insurance or otherwise. For purposes of this subparagraph, amounts paid for menstrual care products shall be treated as paid for medical care.

children.heading: Health insurance may not be purchased from account
children.label: B Health insurance may not be purchased from account
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any payment for insurance.

children.label: i
children.text: a health plan during any period of continuation coverage required under any Federal law,

children.label: ii
children.text: a qualified long-term care insurance contract (as defined in section 7702B(b)),

children.label: iii
children.text: a health plan during a period in which the individual is receiving unemployment compensation under any Federal or State law,

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of an account beneficiary who has attained the age specified in section 1811 of the Social Security Act, any health insurance other than a medicare supplemental policy (as defined in section 1882 of the Social Security Act), or

children.label: v
children.text: any direct primary care service arrangement.

children.heading: Menstrual care product
children.label: D Menstrual care product
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “menstrual care product” means a tampon, pad, liner, cup, sponge, or similar product used by individuals with respect to menstruation or other genital-tract secretions.

children.heading: Account beneficiary
children.label: 3 Account beneficiary
children.text: The term “account beneficiary” means the individual on whose behalf the health savings account was established.

children.label: A
children.text: Section 219(d)(2) (relating to no deduction for rollovers).

children.label: B
children.text: Section 219(f)(3) (relating to time when contributions deemed made).

children.label: C
children.text: Except as provided in section 106(d), section 219(f)(5) (relating to employer payments).

children.label: D
children.text: Section 408(g) (relating to community property laws).

children.label: E
children.text: Section 408(h) (relating to custodial accounts).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A health savings account is exempt from taxation under this subtitle unless such account has ceased to be a health savings account. Notwithstanding the preceding sentence, any such account is subject to the taxes imposed by section 511 (relating to imposition of tax on unrelated business income of charitable, etc. organizations).

children.heading: Account terminations
children.label: 2 Account terminations
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of paragraphs (2) and (4) of section 408(e) shall apply to health savings accounts, and any amount treated as distributed under such rules shall be treated as not used to pay qualified medical expenses.

children.heading: Amounts used for qualified medical expenses
children.label: 1 Amounts used for qualified medical expenses
children.text: Any amount paid or distributed out of a health savings account which is used exclusively to pay qualified medical expenses of any account beneficiary shall not be includible in gross income.

children.heading: Inclusion of amounts not used for qualified medical expenses
children.label: 2 Inclusion of amounts not used for qualified medical expenses
children.text: Any amount paid or distributed out of a health savings account which is not used exclusively to pay the qualified medical expenses of the account beneficiary shall be included in the gross income of such beneficiary.

children.heading: Excess contributions returned before due date of return
children.label: 3 Excess contributions returned before due date of return
i
children.text: such distribution is received by the individual on or before the last day prescribed by law (including extensions of time) for filing such individual’s return for such taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such distribution is accompanied by the amount of net income attributable to such excess contribution.

children.heading: Excess contribution
children.label: B Excess contribution
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “excess contribution” means any contribution (other than a rollover contribution described in paragraph (5) or section 220(f)(5)) which is neither excludable from gross income under section 106(d) nor deductible under this section.

children.heading: Additional tax on distributions not used for qualified medical expenses
children.label: 4 Additional tax on distributions not used for qualified medical expenses

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The tax imposed by this chapter on the account beneficiary for any taxable year in which there is a payment or distribution from a health savings account of such beneficiary which is includible in gross income under paragraph (2) shall be increased by 20 percent of the amount which is so includible.

children.heading: Exception for disability or death
children.label: B Exception for disability or death
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply if the payment or distribution is made after the account beneficiary becomes disabled within the meaning of section 72(m)(7) or dies.

children.heading: Exception for distributions after medicare eligibility
children.label: C Exception for distributions after medicare eligibility
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any payment or distribution after the date on which the account beneficiary attains the age specified in section 1811 of the Social Security Act.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Paragraph (2) shall not apply to any amount paid or distributed from a health savings account to the account beneficiary to the extent the amount received is paid into a health savings account for the benefit of such beneficiary not later than the 60th day after the day on which the beneficiary receives the payment or distribution.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to any amount described in subparagraph (A) received by an individual from a health savings account if, at any time during the 1-year period ending on the day of such receipt, such individual received any other amount described in subparagraph (A) from a health savings account which was not includible in the individual’s gross income because of the application of this paragraph.

children.heading: Coordination with medical expense deduction
children.label: 6 Coordination with medical expense deduction
children.text: For purposes of determining the amount of the deduction under section 213, any payment or distribution out of a health savings account for qualified medical expenses shall not be treated as an expense paid for medical care.

children.heading: Transfer of account incident to divorce
children.label: 7 Transfer of account incident to divorce
children.text: The transfer of an individual’s interest in a health savings account to an individual’s spouse or former spouse under a divorce or separation instrument described in clause (i) of section 121(d)(3)(C) shall not be considered a taxable transfer made by such individual notwithstanding any other provision of this subtitle, and such interest shall, after such transfer, be treated as a health savings account with respect to which such spouse is the account beneficiary.

children.heading: Treatment after death of account beneficiary
children.label: 8 Treatment after death of account beneficiary

children.heading: Treatment if designated beneficiary is spouse
children.label: A Treatment if designated beneficiary is spouse
children.text: If the account beneficiary’s surviving spouse acquires such beneficiary’s interest in a health savings account by reason of being the designated beneficiary of such account at the death of the account beneficiary, such health savings account shall be treated as if the spouse were the account beneficiary.

children.label: I
children.text: such account shall cease to be a health savings account as of the date of death, and

children.label: II
children.text: an amount equal to the fair market value of the assets in such account on such date shall be includible if such person is not the estate of such beneficiary, in such person’s gross income for the taxable year which includes such date, or if such person is the estate of such beneficiary, in such beneficiary’s gross income for the last taxable year of such beneficiary.

children.heading: Reduction of inclusion for predeath expenses
children.label: I Reduction of inclusion for predeath expenses
children.text: The amount includible in gross income under clause (i) by any person (other than the estate) shall be reduced by the amount of qualified medical expenses which were incurred by the decedent before the date of the decedent’s death and paid by such person within 1 year after such date.

children.heading: Deduction for estate taxes
children.label: II Deduction for estate taxes
children.text: An appropriate deduction shall be allowed under section 691(c) to any person (other than the decedent or the decedent’s spouse) with respect to amounts included in gross income under clause (i) by such person.

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: i
children.text: except as provided in clauses (ii) and (iii), “calendar year 1997”,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of each dollar amount in subsection (c)(2)(A), “calendar year 2003”, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of the dollar amount in subsection (c)(1)(E)(ii)(II), “calendar year 2025”.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: 2 Rounding
children.text: If any increase under paragraph (1) is not a multiple of $50, such increase shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of $50.

the trustee of a health savings account to make such reports regarding such account to the Secretary and to the account beneficiary with respect to contributions, distributions, the return of excess contributions, and such other matters as the Secretary determines appropriate, and

any person who provides an individual with a high deductible health plan to make such reports to the Secretary and to the account beneficiary with respect to such plan as the Secretary determines appropriate.
